message:
🐛 fix(drasi): auto-activate first demo seed so header matches rendered pipeline (#8222)

* 🐛 fix(drasi): auto-activate first demo seed so header matches rendered pipeline

Before this fix the connections hook seeded the 4 demo servers but
intentionally left `activeId` empty. Combined with the themed demo
data from #8215, that produced a confusing mismatch: the header
dropdown read "No connection" while the card rendered a full stocks
pipeline underneath it.

Since the themed demo pipelines already match each demo seed, and
`useDrasiResources` short-circuits the fetch for `isDemoSeed: true`
entries, there's no downside to auto-activating the first seed — the
card stays in demo mode, the header now correctly shows which demo
server is active, and the Flow dropdown surfaces the right 3 flows
for the selected theme.

Zero UI / backend changes beyond the seed logic flip.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 fix(drasi): header layout, real confirm modal, +4th source, stream button

Follow-up cluster of fixes that piled up after #8215 landed:

- **Server dropdown no longer eats the header.** Capped at
  min-w-[160px] / max-w-[260px] (was flex-1) so the Flow selector and
  new header-level "Consume stream" button stay anchored next to it
  instead of scrolling off the right edge on wide cards.
- **Real confirm modal for deletes.** User does not want browser
  window.confirm() dialogs. Introduces a single `pendingConfirm`
  state at the card level driving the shared `ConfirmDialog` from
  lib/modals. Both `deleteResource` (source/query/reaction trash) and
  the ConnectionsModal delete flow now open this themed modal instead
  of the native prompt. ConnectionsModal gains an `onRequestRemove`
  callback so the parent owns the confirm UX (the modal itself is
  scoped inside the card container, not portaled).
- **"Consume stream" in the header strip.** The per-query
  results-table placement is easy to miss — the button now also
  lives in the top strip next to the Flow dropdown so users find it
  without having to dig into a selected query.
- **Sources column no longer caps at 3.** The original Wave A render
  had `sources.slice(0, 3)` from when the demo layout was 3 rows
  deep. Creating a 4th source silently dropped it, which was the
  "creating source does not show the new source block" bug. Removed
  the slice — the grid now extends vertically as the user adds
  sources, matching the queries and reactions columns.
- **Default connection = first demo seed.** The previous behavior
  ("No connection" + stocks theme rendered underneath) confused
  users. Auto-activates the first seed in `useDrasiConnections` so
  the header selector matches what the card is actually showing.
  `useDrasiResources` still short-circuits the fetch for demo seeds,
  so the card stays in demo mode.
- New i18n keys: `deleteConfirmTitle`, `deleteConnectionTitle`.

No backend changes.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -136,9 +136,14 @@ function loadState(): StoredState {
       ...c,
       createdAt: now + i,
     }))
-    // Do NOT auto-activate a demo seed — the card should stay in demo mode
-    // until the user explicitly selects one (which still won't go live
-    // because isDemoSeed short-circuits the fetch).
+    // Auto-activate the first demo seed so the header selector matches
+    // the themed pipeline the card is actually rendering. useDrasiResources
+    // short-circuits the fetch when isDemoSeed is set, so the card stays
+    // in demo mode — it just no longer reads "No connection" while a full
+    // pipeline is visible underneath.
+    if (!loaded.activeId) {
+      loaded.activeId = loaded.connections[0].id
+    }
   }
 
   return loaded

@@ -1518,7 +1518,9 @@
     "addSource": "Add Source",
     "addQuery": "Add Query",
     "addReaction": "Add Reaction",
+    "deleteConfirmTitle": "Delete resource",
     "deleteConfirm": "Delete \"{{name}}\"? This cannot be undone.",
+    "deleteConnectionTitle": "Delete Drasi connection",
     "installDrasiTitle": "No live Drasi install detected",
     "installDrasiDescription": "Showing demo data. Install Drasi to visualize your reactive pipelines live.",
     "installDrasiButton": "Install Drasi",
